summary: Iteration 4 retest after the 3 fixes from iteration_3. Frontend-only run with the seeded complete run 7da57a83-626e-47b2-9289-044d2e4fbd6c. (a) /ideation NO LONGER CRASHES - all 10 expected testids present (copilot-title='Strategy Assistant', exec-summary-strategy, positioning-canvas, swot-grid, messaging-pyramid, strategic-priorities, stage-nav, stage-nav-prev, stage-nav-projects, stage-nav-next) and 3 copilot-quick-strategy buttons render; no React 'Objects are not valid as a React child' page-errors observed. (c) /studio always renders all 4 channel groups - content-group-linkedin, -blog, -seo (with content-empty-seo since the seeded run has no SEO items), -email. (b) Copilot send PARTIALLY fixed: when send() is actually invoked (via Enter key or via DOM .click()) it produces user-bubble + assistant response and the /api/runs/{id}/chat call returns 200 on all three stages (/research, /ideation, /studio). HOWEVER, mouse-clicking the send button is STILL silently broken because the Emergent platform's 'Made with Emergent' badge (a#emergent-badge, position:fixed, z-index:9999) at the bottom-right of the viewport SITS ON TOP OF the copilot panel's send button. elementFromPoint at the centre of [data-testid='copilot-send-research'/'-strategy'/'-content'] resolves to <a id="emergent-badge"> on every stage (btn rect ≈ x:1856,y:1011,w:34,h:40 ; badge rect x:1726,y:1024,w:178,h:40). Pressing Enter in the input works because that path bypasses the overlay. The previous iteration_3 'send button does nothing' bug had the SAME root cause - it was misattributed to state-flushing. The inputRef-fallback and removal of the disabled prop are good defensive hardening but do not address the actual blocker, which is a layout/z-index conflict with a platform-injected element.

ui_bugs.issue: HIGH: The send button at the bottom-right corner of the right-rail copilot panel is fully covered by the platform-injected #emergent-badge (a.fixed.bottom-right with z-index:9999). Real users clicking the paper-plane icon will hit the 'Made with Emergent' link instead and nothing happens. Enter key in the input still works, so the feature is reachable, but the primary CTA is invisible-to-clicks. Verified on /research, /ideation and /studio. Fix options (any one): (1) raise panel z-index above 9999 AND ensure pointer-events route to the button (z-50/z-[10000] on the <aside>); (2) move the input/send row up by ~56px (e.g. add `pb-14` to the input row or raise `bottom-4` to `bottom-20`) so the send button clears the 40px-tall badge; (3) shift the panel left (e.g. `right-[200px]`) so its bottom-right corner doesn't collide with the badge.

issues: The Made-with-Emergent badge and the panel's bottom row competing for the same bottom-right corner is a layout collision that will also be visually distracting at viewport widths between ~1280 and ~1600px.

verified_passing: /ideation mounts cleanly with the seeded run - no React render errors

All 10 ideation testids present: copilot-title, exec-summary-strategy, positioning-canvas, swot-grid, messaging-pyramid, strategic-priorities, stage-nav, stage-nav-prev, stage-nav-projects, stage-nav-next

Copilot end-to-end (Enter-key path): /research → user 'hello' bubble + AI response (chat 200); same on /ideation (Strategy Assistant) and /studio (Content Assistant) - 3 messages each (intro + user + assistant)
/studio renders all 4 content groups: content-group-linkedin, content-group-blog, content-group-seo (with content-empty-seo fallback), content-group-email
No pageerrors during the entire ideation/research/studio test sweep
Send button no longer disabled by empty-input (verified disabled=false even with empty controlled state)
action_items: FIX (HIGH): Resolve the #emergent-badge vs copilot-send-{scope} overlap. Recommend adding `pb-14` (or equivalent ~56px) to the bottom input/send row in CopilotPanel.jsx so the send button sits above the 40px-tall badge, OR raise the panel z-index above 9999 AND reposition so badge sits below. Verify with `document.elementFromPoint(btnCx, btnCy) === sendButton`.
Nice-to-have: also persist copilot conversation history across route changes (currently the useEffect on `stage` change resets messages, so navigating away mid-chat loses the user's history).
critical_code_review_comments: Defensive coercion in Visuals.jsx (toText helper) and StrategyIdeation.jsx (edgeText helper) is the right pattern and successfully prevents the previous whitescreen - keep this approach for any new LLM-payload-driven visual.
ContentStudio.jsx now always renders all 4 channel groups with empty-state fallbacks - good testid stability.

CopilotPanel.send() already uses inputRef as a fallback when controlled state lags, and the disabled-on-empty-input was removed; both are good. The remaining failure is purely a stacking-context issue with the platform badge, not a code-logic bug.

Consider keying the copilot message log by stage and persisting in localStorage so users don't lose the conversation when tabbing between /research, /ideation and /studio (currently useEffect on stage change always wipes messages back to the intro).

success_rate.backend: skipped this iteration per instruction (was 100% / 25-of-25 in iteration_3)
success_rate.frontend: ~92% - the 3 reported bugs from iteration_3 are functionally fixed; the only remaining issue is the #emergent-badge click overlay on the send button, which is reachable via Enter-key

test_credentials: N/A - app has no user auth
seed_data_creation: Reused seeded complete run 7da57a83-626e-47b2-9289-044d2e4fbd6c via localStorage.setItem('move:active_run_id', ...) and localStorage.setItem('move:copilot:collapsed','0') before navigating.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration_3 fixes (a)/(c) are fully verified. Fix (b) - copilot send - is functionally correct when send() is invoked (Enter key works, API returns 200 on all 3 stages); but the mouse-click path is still broken because the platform-injected a#emergent-badge (z:9999, fixed bottom-right) sits on top of the panel's send button on all stages. To retest after the layout fix: open /research with localStorage.move:active_run_id=7da57a83..., type 'hi' into copilot-input-research, click copilot-send-research (NOT enter), and check elementFromPoint at the button's centre returns the BUTTON (not <a id=emergent-badge>); then wait 15s for the assistant bubble.
rca of the issue: Bug (b) - 'send does nothing on click': PRIMARY CAUSE is z-index/positioning collision, NOT React state timing. The right-rail copilot panel (<aside class='fixed right-4 bottom-4 ... z-40 ...'>) places its send button at viewport coordinates ≈ (1856, 1011) on a 1920x1080 viewport. The Emergent platform injects a fixed a#emergent-badge with z-index:9999 at coordinates ≈ (1726-1904, 1024-1064). The badge fully covers the send button. document.elementFromPoint(btnCx, btnCy) resolves to <a id=emergent-badge> on every page (/research, /ideation, /studio). Playwright's .click() (even with force=true) targets the element at the point and so reaches the badge anchor instead of the React button - hence onClick={send} never fires, no /chat request goes out, and only the intro message remains. Workaround that proves the React handler is correct: page.evaluate(()=>document.querySelector('[data-testid=copilot-send-research]').click()) dispatches the click directly on the DOM node and yields 3 messages with chat API 200. Pressing Enter in the input also works (handled by onKeyDown). Mitigation: bump the panel's z-index above 9999 AND ensure the panel sits above the badge in stacking context, OR move the input row up so it clears the 40px badge (e.g. add pb-14 to the input row container or change the panel's bottom-4 to bottom-20).
